name: Lorenzo Bianchi Chignoli
bio: I am a PhD Candidate in Economics at Universitat Pompeu Fabra and Barcelona School of Economics, and a Research Intern at the Bank of Italy (Monetary Policy Strategy, Economic Outlook and Monetary Policy Directorate). I work on Macroeconomics, in particular New Keynesian theory, macrofinance, and monetary policy transmission, and I often solve my models with deep nerual networks.

bio.title: Research Agenda
bio.text: My research agenda focuses on the supply-side transmission of monetary policy. I tackle this through a New Keynesian and New Monetarist perspective: modeling goods and credit markets with real rigidities, where quantity rationing is crucial for equilibrium, and such that non-Walrasian market clearing can occur.

bio: I earned my degree in Economics at the University of Pavia as a Ghislieri College Fellow and IUSS Scholar. Outside of academia, I run, make music, and prove you need not be good at chess to enjoy it.
I also publish some lecture notes and materials from my PhD courses on my Economics Notebook!

contact.address: C. Ramon Trias Fargas, 25-27 | 08005 Barcelona, Spain
